data.title: Sturm, Ruger shares slip as CEO flags short-term profit hit from reorganization
data.description:
Shares of Sturm, Ruger (NYSE:RGR) are on the defensive and expected to snap a six-day winning streak on Friday after the CEO warned that restructuring costs and initiatives to improve long-term growth will cost the company $15M to $20M over the remainder of 2025.

In a letter to employees marking his 100th day as CEO, Todd Seyfert noted the reorganization of the company to create autonomous business units resulted in the loss of jobs throughout the organization. Next comes the financial costs tied to this corporate realignment, specifically severance and separation costs of ~$3M to be recognized over the coming quarters.

To retire excess, obsolete, or ready to be discontinued product, Sturm, Ruger (NYSE:RGR) expects a non-cash cost of $10M to $15M in Q2, while “product repositioning” to reach customers will require a “better price point” which will “come at the expense of profitability” in the short-term.

“We expect our overall gross margin to decrease during the remainder of the year while we reposition these lines in the market, while still working to increase production,” Seyfert said in his letter.

These measures are expected to be completed by the beginning of 2026, but in the meantime, results for the remainder of this year – before the one-time costs and expenses – will look more like prior periods, such as the latter of 2019.

During the company’s most recent earnings call, Seyfert noted the challenging environment across the firearm industry requiring Sturm, Ruger (NYSE:RGR) to scale operations in alignment with market needs.

“Our focus is clear, invest in our culture people and organizational efficiency, expand our production capabilities to meet product specific demand, deliver safe, reliable and innovative products for our consumers,” Seyfert said on the call.

data.title: Boeing 787 fleet likely to be grounded in India during investigations
data.description:
Boeing (NYSE:BA) -1.8% pre-market Friday following the stock's nearly 5% drop in the previous session, as reports indicate India's government likely will ground the Dreamliner 787-8 fleet for a safety review, a day after one of the aircraft supplied to Air India crashed shortly takeoff, killing more than 240 people.

Air India also is likely to face scrutiny over its standard operating procedure on aircraft maintenance; the carrier has not suffered crashes in its recent past, but it has been working to overcome a legacy of unprofitability and a reputation for shoddy service.

An early focus of an investigation is expected to focus on whether the aircraft had a loss or reduction in engine thrust as a factor in the crash, although many questions remain unanswered; CNN reported that the crash was not caused by a bird strike or overloading.

India's civil aviation minister said at least one of the plane's black boxes was recovered from the wreckage.

U.S. Secretary of Transportation Sean Duffy and Acting Federal Aviation Administration head Chris Rocheleau said late Thursday they have not yet seen any safety data that would suggest the need for an immediate halt Boeing (NYSE:BA) 787 flights in the U.S.

Duffy said he had spoken to National Transportation Safety Board Chair Jennifer Homendy, and a team of NTSB and FAA officials, with support from Boeing (BA) and engine manufacturer GE Aerospace (GEV), would be going to India.

data.title: First Bancorp raises dividend by 4.5% to $0.23
data.description:
• First Bancorp (NASDAQ:FBNC) declares $0.23/share quarterly dividend, 4.5% increase from prior dividend of $0.22.
  • Forward yield 2.22%
  • Payable July 25; for shareholders of record June 30; ex-div June 30.
  • See FBNC Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Bausch Health ticks higher following large director stock purchase
data.description:
• Bausch Health Companies (NYSE:BHC) is up ~5% in premarket trading Friday following the disclosure that director John Paulson recently bought close to $15M worth of shares through his firm Paulson Capital.
  • An SEC filing shows three purchases made June 10, 11, and 12. The number of shares purchased each day, was, respectively, ~754K, 1.029M, and 1.005M. Shares were bought at $5.05-$5.47, making the entire purchase worth ~$14.7M.
  • Paulson currently serves as non-executive chairperson on Bausch's board.

data.title: Chanson International announces pricing of $8 million public offering
data.description:
• Chanson International (NASDAQ:CHSN) announced on Friday that it has priced a public offering, with gross proceeds to the company expected to be approximately $8 million.
  • The offering consists of 16 million units consisting of one Class A ordinary share, or in lieu thereof, a pre-funded warrant, one series A warrant to purchase one Class A Ordinary share and one series B warrant to purchase one Class A Ordinary share.
  • The public offering price of the units is $0.50 per unit, said the company, adding that, "each of the Series A Warrants and the Series B Warrants will have an exercise price of $0.525 per Class A Ordinary Share and be exercisable beginning on the date of the issuance date and ending on the two and half anniversary of the issuance date."
  • The offering is expected to close on or about June 16, 2025.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from this offering to open new stores in China and in the U.S., and the specific allocation of net proceeds to each market will be based on market conditions.
  • CHSN -25.2% premarket to $0.4114.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Viasat to receive $568M under Ligado agreement
data.description:
• Viasat (NASDAQ:VSAT) expects to receive $568M from Ligado Networks in fiscal year 2026 under a term sheet that settles its subsidiary Inmarsat Global’s opposition to Ligado’s planned restructuring.
  • Starting on September 30, 2025, Ligado will start making quarterly payments of ~$16M per quarter to Inmarsat. The quarterly payment amount increases 3% per year for the life of the contract (through 2107).
  • Ligado will make two lump sum payments to Inmarsat: $420M on October 31, 2025, followed by $100M on March 31, 2026. Viasat will use the funds primarily to manage near term maturities and address its extended maturity profile.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Schwab total client assets rise 5% M/M to $10.35T in May
data.description:
• Charles Schwab (NYSE:SCHW) on Friday said its total client assets rose 12% Y/Y and 5%M/M to $10.35 trillion as of month-end May.
  • Core net new assets brought to the company by new and existing clients increased 13% Y/Y to $35.0 billion in May.
  • New brokerage accounts opened during the month totaled 336,000, up 7% versus May 2024.
  • May trading volumes exceeding 7 million daily average trades for the 5th consecutive month, said the company.
  • Transactional sweep cash declined by $5.2 billion to end May at $398.1 billion.
  • The company said, "inclusive of clients’ engagement with markets and April tax seasonality, quarter-to-date transactional sweep cash is down less than 3% from March 2025 levels, helping us further reduce high-cost funding at the banks to approximately $32 billion as of May month-end."
  • SCHW -1.97% premarket to $86.75.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Mitsubishi UFJ Financial announces $600M loan to aircraft lessor Aircastle
data.description:
Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group (OTCPK:MBFJF) announced on Friday the close of a $600M unsecured term loan provided to the aircraft lessor, Aircastle.

The loan is expected to help Aircastle's working capital needs for general corporate purposes, including aircraft acquisitions, through April 2030.

Shares were -0.99% pre-market to $13.45.

data.title: SharpLink Gaming acquires ETH worth $463M; shares down 68%
data.description:
• SharpLink Gaming (NASDAQ:SBET) announced the strategic acquisition of 176,270.69 ETH for an aggregate purchase price of $462.95M, at an average acquisition price of $2,626 per ETH.
  • This acquisition positions SBET as the largest publicly-traded holder of ETH in the world, and the second-largest holder behind the Ethereum Foundation.
  • SBET also disclosed it raised an additional $79M in approximate gross proceeds through its at-the-market facility.
  • With a majority of the ATM proceeds used to fund further purchases of ETH, contributing to the total above, these collective purchases have generated ETH per share growth of 11.8% since June 2, 2025.
  • As of June 13, 2025, over 95% of SharpLink’s ETH holdings are actively deployed in staking and liquid staking solutions.
  • SBET shares slump 68% premarket on Friday.
  • Source: Press release
